title: Feature: Add outreach follow-up tracking (reply status + due-cadence queue)
summary: Add a per-posting outreach status (awaiting/replied/no-response) distinct from the application response axis, plus a time-based follow-up cadence, surfaced in a dedicated Follow-ups view, so the user can see who replied and who has gone cold and needs a nudge. Detection is manual; scout never reads email.
outOfScope: Auto-detecting replies from email/Gmail or any inbox integration — reply status is set manually only
Per-contact or per-draft reply tracking — outreach_status is a single value per posting, not per recipient or per sent draft
Notifications, push, or email reminders for due follow-ups — the queue is pull-only (the user opens the Follow-ups view)
Changes to the outreach writing pipeline — drafting an actual follow-up email reuses the existing regenerate -> mark-sent flow unchanged
Changes to the application `response` axis (screening/interview/offer/rejected) semantics — outreach_status is a separate, parallel axis
Auto-stop after K follow-ups — while `awaiting`, a posting re-surfaces every interval until manually moved to replied/no_response (confirmed intended)
technicalConsiderations: Schema: add `outreach_status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT ''` to job_postings via the next sequential migration (latest is ~M44; implementer confirms the next number). Empty string = none; app-level validation restricts to {'', 'awaiting', 'replied', 'no_response'}.

outreach_status is a SEPARATE axis from the existing `response` field. A posting can be `awaiting` reply with `response` still empty. The follow-up 'due' computation excludes postings whose `response` is non-empty (already engaged) or that are hidden/rejected.

Reuse `store.PostingTracking` + `UpdatePostingTracking` (internal/store/postings.go) and the existing `PUT /api/postings/{id}` handler (internal/web/capture.go) — add the new field rather than a new endpoint for status edits.

Coupling point: `MarkOutreachDraftSent` (internal/store/outreach_drafts.go ~lines 237-269) already bumps outreach_count, sets last_outreach_at, and clears next_up_at in one transaction. It should also set outreach_status -> 'awaiting' (unless already 'replied').

Cadence: a DB-backed singleton setting `follow_up_interval_days` (default 7), following the existing singleton pattern (taste_filter / email-template / prompt_overrides). 'Due' = outreach_status='awaiting' AND last_outreach_at <= today - interval AND response='' AND not hidden/rejected, sorted most-overdue-first.

Web UI is a Vite + vanilla-TS app in web/src/app.ts consuming @brainbot/web-toolkit, built to internal/web/dist and go:embed-ed. Touch points: renderJobs (jobs table), renderPursuit (slide-in panel), and a new Follow-ups view alongside the companies|jobs tabs.
Build caveat (per project memory): `npm run build` is broken in this sandbox — use the tsx workaround + docker compose redeploy when verifying the UI stories (US-004, US-005).
north-star.md is canonical architecture: verdicts stay scout-local, this is purely a tracking concern on the application-tracker side (job_postings), no brain writes.
